# middleware flag: routes function takes func(next http.Handler) http.Handler
# and wraps every registered handler; nil middleware means no wrapping

muxt generate --output-routes-func-with-middleware-param
muxt check

exec go test -count=1

-- go.mod --
module example.com

go 1.24
-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templateFiles embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templateFiles, "*.gohtml"))
-- template.gohtml --
{{define "GET /"}}Hello, world!{{end}}
-- template_test.go --
package server

import (
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestMiddlewareWrapsHandlers(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	TemplateRoutes(mux, nil, func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
		return http.HandlerFunc(func(res http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
			res.Header().Set("x-middleware", req.Pattern)
			next.ServeHTTP(res, req)
		})
	})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
	res := rec.Result()

	if res.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		t.Error("status =", res.StatusCode, "want", http.StatusOK)
	}
	if got, want := res.Header.Get("x-middleware"), "GET /"; got != want {
		t.Errorf("x-middleware header = %q, want %q", got, want)
	}
	body, _ := io.ReadAll(res.Body)
	if !strings.Contains(string(body), "Hello, world!") {
		t.Errorf("body %q does not contain greeting", body)
	}
}

func TestNilMiddleware(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	TemplateRoutes(mux, nil, nil)

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if got := rec.Result().StatusCode; got != http.StatusOK {
		t.Error("status with nil middleware =", got, "want", http.StatusOK)
	}
}
